sources.title: Biggest City in the World by Population 2025 | Statistics
Africa’s Largest City: Lagos, Cairo, or Kinshasa? The Urban Powerhouses

output.findings: Lagos has an estimated population of about 15.9 million in 2025 with an annual growth rate of approximately 2.48% to 3.78% depending on the source and year referenced. The city is recognized as one of the fastest-growing megacities with growth rates cited up to around 3.7% per year. Cairo's metro area population is about 23 to 23.5 million as of 2025-2026, with a growth rate near 2% annually. Kinshasa's population is estimated around 16.3 million in 2025 with some of the highest urban growth rates globally, reportedly reaching over 4% per year in some analyses. Among Lagos, Cairo, and Kinshasa, Kinshasa is generally considered to be growing fastest by percentage growth rate, followed by Lagos, then Cairo.

output.findings: Lagos metro area population in 2023 was approximately 15.95 million with an annual growth rate around 3.63% to 3.78% based on MacroTrends data. Another source estimates Lagos population around 14.88 million in 2026 with a 2.48% annual growth rate. Cairo metro area population in 2023 was about 22.18 million with a growth rate near 1.99% increase from 2022 according to MacroTrends. Kinshasa metro population was about 16.32 million in 2023 with an estimated growth rate of approximately 4.4% increase from the previous year. The city is noted as one of the fastest-growing urban centers in Africa. Comparing these, Kinshasa is growing fastest, followed by Lagos, then Cairo.

metadata.label: compare
metadata.question: Determine which city has the highest growth rate and prepare a summary

output.rationale: Extracts the given populations and growth rates, prints them, and selects the city with the highest growth percentage.

output.final_answer: Lagos has a population of 15.95 million with a growth rate of 3.63%, Cairo has 22.18 million with a growth rate of 1.99%, and Kinshasa has 16.32 million with a growth rate of 4.40%. Kinshasa is growing fastest, with a growth rate of 4.40%.
